163	// TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA: the worker observes cancel and returns within
164	// a small multiple of CancelPollInterval (the SLA contract — ADR-022 §Cancel
165	// SLA promises observation within one poll interval at the next safe point).
166	func TestCancelLatency_UnderSLA(t *testing.T) {
167		const beadID = "ddx-cancel-sla"
168		const pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
169	
170		prev := CancelPollInterval
171		CancelPollInterval = pollInterval
172		t.Cleanup(func() { CancelPollInterval = prev })
173	
174		projectRoot := setupArtifactTestProjectRoot(t)
175		gitOps := &artifactTestGitOps{
176			projectRoot: projectRoot,
177			baseRev:     "sla0000000000001",
178			resultRev:   "sla0000000000001",
179			wtSetupFn: func(wtPath string) {
180				setupArtifactTestWorktree(t, wtPath, beadID, "", false, 0)
181			},
182		}
183	
184		cancelStore := &fakeCancelStore{}
185		runner := newBlockingAgentRunner()
186	
187		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
188	
189		startedDispatch := make(chan struct{})
190		done := make(chan *ExecuteBeadResult, 1)
191		go func() {
192			close(startedDispatch)
193			res, _ := ExecuteBeadWithConfig(context.Background(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, ExecuteBeadRuntime{
194				AgentRunner: runner,
195				BeadCancel:  cancelStore,
196			}, gitOps)
197			done <- res
198		}()
199	
200		<-startedDispatch
201		<-runner.startedAt
202		cancelStore.request()
203		cancelAt := time.Now()
204	
205		select {
206		case res := <-done:
207			latency := time.Since(cancelAt)
208			// Allow up to 4× the poll interval to absorb scheduler + worktree
209			// teardown overhead. The SLA contract is "within one poll interval at
210			// the next safe point"; this test asserts the bound is honored, not
211			// the wall-clock minimum.
212			if latency > 4*pollInterval+250*time.Millisecond {
213				t.Errorf("cancel latency %v exceeded SLA budget (%v)", latency, 4*pollInterval+250*time.Millisecond)
214			}

20	func newCancelTestBeadStore(t *testing.T, projectRoot, beadID string) *bead.Store {
21		t.Helper()

32	// fakeCancelStore is a memory-only BeadCancelStore for worker tests. The
33	// trigger channel pings the poll loop the moment the test wants the marker
34	// observed (instead of waiting for the next CancelPollInterval tick to fire
35	// against a real bead store on disk).
36	type fakeCancelStore struct {
37		mu        sync.Mutex
38		requested bool
39		honored   bool
40		calls     atomic.Int32
41	}
42	
43	func (f *fakeCancelStore) IsCancelRequested(id string) (bool, error) {
44		f.calls.Add(1)
45		f.mu.Lock()
46		defer f.mu.Unlock()
47		if f.honored {
48			return false, nil
49		}
50		return f.requested, nil
51	}
52	
53	func (f *fakeCancelStore) MarkCancelHonored(id string) error {
54		f.mu.Lock()
55		defer f.mu.Unlock()
56		f.honored = true
57		return nil
58	}

14	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted is an end-to-end integration test that
15	// drives runAgentExecuteBead via a cobra Command.Execute call and asserts that
16	// routing evidence (resolved_provider, resolved_model, route_reason) is persisted
17	// as a kind:routing bead event in the bead store.
18	//
19	// This test exercises the full pipeline:
20	//
21	//	CLI flags → ExecuteBeadOptions → AgentRunner.Run → appendBeadRoutingEvidence → bead store
22	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidencePersisted(t *testing.T) {
23		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
24			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
25			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222", // agent produced a commit
26			mergeErr:    nil,
27		}
28		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
29			ExitCode:        0,
30			Harness:         "mock-harness",
31			Provider:        "test-provider",
32			Model:           "test-model-7b",
33			RouteReason:     "catalog-match",
34			ResolvedBaseURL: "http://test.example.com/v1",
35		}}
36		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
37	
38		// Drive the full command via cobra Command.Execute — not by calling internal
39		// functions directly. This exercises the CLI flag → ExecuteBeadOptions path.
40		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
41	
42		// Sanity-check: the command itself succeeded.
43		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
44		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
45		assert.Equal(t, "test-provider", res.Provider)
46		assert.Equal(t, "test-model-7b", res.Model)
47	
48		// Read routing events directly from the bead store that runAgentExecuteBead
49		// created (backed by f.WorkingDir/.ddx). This verifies end-to-end persistence,
50		// not just that appendBeadRoutingEvidence was called in isolation.
51		store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, ".ddx"))
52		routingEvents, err := store.EventsByKind("my-bead", "routing")
53		require.NoError(t, err)
54		require.Len(t, routingEvents, 1, "expected exactly one kind:routing event")
55	
56		evt := routingEvents[0]
57		assert.Equal(t, "routing", evt.Kind)
58		assert.Equal(t, "ddx", evt.Actor)
59		assert.Equal(t, "ddx agent execute-bead", evt.Source)
60		assert.NotEmpty(t, evt.Summary)
61	
62		// Parse the JSON body and assert all three required routing fields.
63		var body struct {
64			ResolvedProvider string `json:"resolved_provider"`
65			ResolvedModel    string `json:"resolved_model"`
66			RouteReason      string `json:"route_reason"`
67			BaseURL          string `json:"base_url"`
68		}
69		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(evt.Body), &body),
70			"routing event body should be valid JSON: %s", evt.Body)
71		assert.Equal(t, "test-provider", body.ResolvedProvider)
72		assert.Equal(t, "test-model-7b", body.ResolvedModel)
73		assert.Equal(t, "catalog-match", body.RouteReason)
74		assert.Equal(t, "http://test.example.com/v1", body.BaseURL)
75	}

77	// TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceProviderFallsBackToHarness verifies that when
78	// the runner returns an empty Provider, the routing event's resolved_provider
79	// falls back to the harness name (as implemented in appendBeadRoutingEvidence).
80	func TestExecuteBeadRoutingEvidenceProviderFallsBackToHarness(t *testing.T) {
81		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
82			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
83			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
84		}
85		// Runner returns no Provider — harness should be used as resolved_provider.
86		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{
87			ExitCode:    0,
88			Harness:     "fallback-harness",
89			Provider:    "", // empty — triggers harness fallback
90			Model:       "fallback-model",
91			RouteReason: "first-available",
92		}}
93		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
94	
95		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
96		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
97

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(cmd): isolate TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough from real fizeau providers [ddx-11f0cbf2]

The pre-dispatch lint hook calls dispatchViaResolvedConfig with svc=nil,
runner=nil, which falls through to NewServiceFromWorkDir → fizeau loads
~/.config/fizeau/config.yaml and dispatches to real providers (HTTP or
exec-based). In the test environment this causes TCP timeouts or long-
running exec subprocesses that block the entire cmd package.

Fix: redirect HOME to t.TempDir() (prevents global fizeau config from
loading) and set factory.AgentRunnerOverride to the existing
tryHookRunnerStub (routes lint/triage hook dispatch through the stub
instead of fizeau).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Both fixes are committed:
1. **`cli/internal/agent/runner_test.go`** (checkpoint 6538e31): `TestRunLMStudioDispatchesThroughEmbeddedAgent` and `TestRunOpenRouterDispatchesThroughEmbeddedAgent` — added `t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())`, `WorkDir: t.TempDir()`, and `context.WithTimeout(5s)` to prevent fizeau from loading the real global provider config and making TCP connections.

2. **`cli/cmd/try_test.go`** (f61c325): `TestTry_FlagsPlumbThrough` — added `t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())` and `factory.AgentRunnerOverride = &tryHookRunnerStub{t: t}` so the pre-dispatch lint hook routes through a stub runner instead of creating a real fizeau service.

Root cause in both cases: `dispatchViaResolvedConfig` creates a fizeau service via `NewServiceFromWorkDir` when both `svc` and `runner` are nil. Fizeau loads `~/.config/fizeau/config.yaml` (which has real providers in this environment), then attempts TCP connections that block the test.
